skill.name: create-pr
skill.description: Generates pull request content from Git branch changes, including a commit message, PR title, and PR body based on the repository template.
skill.summary: Create commit messages, PR titles, and PR bodies from repository diffs.
skill.author: Cloud-Officer

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command execution patterns in SKILL.md. Review confirms these commands are mostly expected Git and template-inspection steps for pull request generation, but they still require shell execution, repository diff access, filesystem reads, and a named environment variable read. The weak cryptography alerts appear to be false positives because no cryptographic algorithm use is present in the cited lines.

medium_findings.title: Required Shell Execution for Repository Inspection
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run Git and shell commands to inspect the current branch, changed files, full diffs, and PR templates. This is necessary for the stated workflow, but it gives the skill command execution over the user repository context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain direct shell commands that the skill tells the agent to execute. The commands are aligned with pull request generation, so the concern is capability exposure rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Named Environment Variable Read
medium_findings.description: The skill reads JIRA_TICKET from the environment and may insert it into pull request content. This is limited to a specific ticket variable, but environment reads should be disclosed because misconfigured variables can contain sensitive values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence directly shows echoing $JIRA_TICKET and later using that value in output. The variable name suggests legitimate workflow metadata, but it is still environment access.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Read Limited to PR Templates
low_findings.description: The skill reads .github pull request template files to fill the PR body. This is expected behavior for this skill and does not show broad filesystem harvesting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited command targets two specific PR template paths and has a fallback message. No evidence found of reading unrelated files or secret locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on lines that describe PR text and summary formatting. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic algorithm use in the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language instructions about pull request content. There is no cryptographic code or algorithm reference in those locations.

low_findings.title: Repository Reconnaissance Is Workflow-Scoped
low_findings.description: The skill gathers branch names, diff stats, and full diffs to summarize code changes. This is repository inspection, but it matches the declared purpose and does not include network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands gather repository metadata and diffs, which the skill needs to create PR content. No evidence found of sending the data outside the local assistant workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Diff Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The DEFAULT_BRANCH variable is populated through shell command substitution before running git diff. This appears legitimate, but command substitution increases review burden for marketplace users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present and executable, but it derives from Git remote HEAD metadata and is used for expected diff selection. No clear command injection path is proven from the skill text alone.

dangerous_patterns.title: Full Repository Diff Exposure to the Agent
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requests the full committed and staged diff before generating PR text. This can expose proprietary code to the model context, although it is necessary for accurate PR summaries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly requests the full diff. The privacy concern is clear, while the use remains aligned with the stated pull request workflow.

content.value_statement: Writing clear pull request content from raw diffs takes time and can miss important files. This skill inspects Git changes and prepares a commit message, PR title, and template-based PR body.
actual_capabilities: Reads the current Git branch name before drafting PR content.
Generates a concise commit message from repository changes.
Creates a PR title that summarizes the branch purpose.
Uses repository pull request templates when they are present.
Preserves template checkbox items while marking relevant options.
Includes a Jira ticket when JIRA_TICKET is set and the template supports it.
limitations: Requires access to Git commands in the target repository.
May expose full diffs to the assistant context during generation.
Does not create or submit the pull request to a hosting service.
Depends on clear diffs and templates for accurate output.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature PR
use_cases.description: Generate a commit message, title, and PR body after finishing a feature branch.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team PRs
use_cases.description: Fill the existing repository PR template with consistent summaries and checkbox handling.

use_cases.title: Document Maintenance Changes
use_cases.description: Summarize refactors, dependency updates, and cleanup work before requesting review.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Basic PR Content
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer wants a complete first draft from current changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create PR content for my current branch using the repository template.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Review Clarity
prompt_templates.scenario: A reviewer needs a concise summary of important changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create PR content and make the summary easy for reviewers to scan.

prompt_templates.title: Include Jira Context
prompt_templates.scenario: The repository template includes a Jira ticket section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create PR content and use JIRA_TICKET if the template includes a Jira section.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Complex Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: The branch includes migrations, breaking changes, or reprocessing needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create PR content and call out any migration, breaking change, or reprocessing impact.

output_examples.input: A branch adds user profile validation and updates related tests.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a short commit message, a focused PR title, and a PR body that lists validation and test changes.

output_examples.input: A repository has a pull request template with type and checklist sections.
output_examples.output: The skill fills the template, preserves checkbox items, and marks only the options that match the diff.

output_examples.input: A branch contains a database migration and application code updates.
output_examples.output: The skill adds migration context in the PR body and highlights reviewer actions related to data changes.

best_practices: Review generated PR content before publishing it.
Run tests and linters before asking the skill to draft final content.
Keep JIRA_TICKET limited to public ticket identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not run it in repositories with secrets in diffs unless disclosure is acceptable.
Do not treat generated checkbox selections as a substitute for human review.
Do not use it to submit PRs without checking template requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill open a pull request?
faq.answer: No. It drafts the commit message, PR title, and PR body for you to review and submit.

faq.question: Does it read my full code changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It requests Git diff output so it can summarize changed files accurately.

faq.question: Does it use pull request templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks common .github pull request template paths and fills the template when present.

faq.question: Can it include Jira tickets?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use the JIRA_TICKET environment variable when the template contains a Jira section.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing the output?
faq.answer: Confirm the summary, checkbox selections, migration notes, and any ticket references are correct.
